Active Development

The Lab

Works in progress. Experimental tools and visualizations being built in the open. Some content is locked while under active construction — check back as the site evolves.

LAB POLICY
Transparency first. Every tool in the Lab is shown in its actual current state. Locked cards indicate work genuinely in progress — not vaporware. Progress bars reflect real completion estimates.
In Development

What’s being built

These are the next major tools on the Healthcare Uncharted roadmap. Each one is in active development.

In Progress
Gap
U.S. Healthcare Gap Map
An interactive D3.js + TopoJSON choropleth map of U.S. healthcare resource inequality. State-level data on physician workforce shortages, rural-urban access divides, ESRD prevalence, and disparity indices.
Research & data75%
Visualization build40%
D3.jsTopoJSONUSRDSDisparities
🔒
In development
In Progress
4P
4Ps Layered System Map
The full navigational spine of Healthcare Uncharted. A layered D3.js workflow diagram of the entire healthcare system — Patient, Provider, Payer, and Policy Maker lanes with clickable nodes linking to tools and articles.
Architecture design85%
D3.js build20%
D3.js4PsNavigationInteractive
🔒
In development
In Progress
Dx
Clinical Operations Split: Dialysis Case Study
An interactive visualization exploring the two-sublane split between Clinical Medical (2a) and Clinical Operations (2b) using dialysis as a worked example. Confirms the handoff between care delivery, charge capture, supply, and staffing.
Clinical research90%
Visualization55%
Clinical OpsDialysisESRDCharge Capture
🔒
In development
Active
Ref
HIT Terminology Reference — Expanded
A searchable, filterable crosswalk of Epic, Cerner, and FHIR terminology. EPT vs Person. FLO vs DTA. CSN vs encntr_id. FSD vs clinical_event. Currently available in the Learn section — expanded version in development.
Core content60%
EpicCernerFHIRSearchable
A living resource for clinical informatics, healthcare data architecture, and the real story behind the systems that run modern medicine.

Built by David Eitel, RRT, MHA, MSRT, RRT-ACCS

Active development
Source Policy
All vendor-specific terminology, technical specifications, and system references on this site are derived from publicly available documentation — including fhir.epic.com, fhir.cerner.com, HL7 FHIR R4, ONC HealthIT.gov, and HHS.gov. Each factual claim is linked to its source. This site does not reproduce proprietary materials, internal vendor training content, or licensed documentation.
